---
jupyter: python3
keep-ipynb: true
format:
  html:
    other-links:
      - text: Download Notebook
        icon: journal-code
        href: 2_ecosystem_overview.ipynb
title: "Terrestrial Ecosystems of {{< meta country-name >}}"
numbering:
  figure:
    enumerator: 2.%s
---

```{python}
#| label: import-modules
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import os
from pprint import pprint

import geopandas as gpd
import yaml

import rle.core
import iucn_get_data
from iucn_get_data import Typology

print(f'{rle.core.__version__ = }')
print(f'{iucn_get_data.__version__ = }')
```

```{python}
#| label: load-country-config
#| echo: false
#| output: false
# Load up the country-level configuration.
with open('../config/country_config.yaml', 'r') as f:
    config = yaml.safe_load(f)
pprint(config)
```

```{python}
#| label: load-ecosystems-data
#| echo: false
#| output: false
# Load the country's ecosystems data as a GeoDataFrame.
#
# Column roles come from the config. The functional-group column is optional:
# when it is not configured (or not present in the data), the GET typology
# output (functional group, biome, realm) is omitted below.
src = config['ecosystem_source']
# ecosystem_code_column is optional: fall back to the name column.
ecosystem_column = src.get('ecosystem_code_column') or src.get('ecosystem_name_column')  # e.g. ECO_CODE
ecosystem_name_column = src.get('ecosystem_name_column')   # e.g. ECO_NAME
functional_group_column = src.get('functional_group_column')  # e.g. EFG1 or None

eco = rle.core.Ecosystems.from_file(
    src['data'],
    ecosystem_column=ecosystem_column,
    ecosystem_name_column=ecosystem_name_column,
    functional_group_column=functional_group_column,
)
ecosystems_df = eco.to_geodataframe()

# The functional-group column is present only when it is both configured and
# actually in the loaded data.
has_functional_group = (
    functional_group_column is not None
    and functional_group_column in ecosystems_df.columns
)
```

```{python}
#| label: functional-group-column-warning
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
# Surface a visible warning when a functional-group column is configured but
# absent from the data. Without this, the GET-typology sections below are
# omitted silently, so a typo (or a dataset with no GET crosswalk) looks
# indistinguishable from an intentional choice.
if functional_group_column is not None and not has_functional_group:
    available = ", ".join(map(str, ecosystems_df.columns))
    print(
        "::: {.callout-warning}\n"
        "## Functional-group column not found\n\n"
        f"The configured `functional_group_column` "
        f"(`{functional_group_column}`) is not a column in the ecosystem "
        "data, so the IUCN Global Ecosystem Typology sections (functional "
        "group, biome, realm) are omitted from this chapter.\n\n"
        "Set `functional_group_column` in `config/country_config.yaml` to a "
        "valid column (or remove it if the data has no GET crosswalk). "
        f"Available columns: {available}\n"
        ":::\n"
    )
```

```{python}
#| label: calculate-ecosystem-type-and-biome-counts
#| echo: false
ecosystem_type_count = len(eco.unique_ecosystems())
biome_count = len(eco.unique_functional_groups()) if has_functional_group else None
```


```{python}
#| label: ecosystem-overview-narrative
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
# Build the overview text from the data. The GET-typology sentences (biome
# count, IUCN GET crosswalk, realm/biome/functional-group hierarchy) are
# included only when a functional-group column is present.
country = config['country_name']

sentences = []
if has_functional_group:
    sentences.append(
        f"The {country} ecosystem typology includes {ecosystem_type_count} "
        f"ecosystem types across {biome_count} biomes (@tbl-typology)."
    )
    sentences.append(
        "The ecosystem typology is consistent with the IUCN global ecosystem "
        "typology [@keith2020get] to support crosswalks and comparisons with "
        "Red Lists of ecosystems in other countries and regions."
    )
    sentences.append(
        f"The {country} ecosystem typology has a hierarchical structure, where "
        "each ecosystem type is assigned to a realm, biome and functional group "
        "(ecotype). Realm refers to one of the four component media in the "
        "biosphere, biome is the segment of the biosphere united by major "
        "functional traits and macro-environmental features, and functional "
        "group is a group of related ecosystems within a biome."
    )
else:
    sentences.append(
        f"The {country} ecosystem typology includes {ecosystem_type_count} "
        f"ecosystem types (@tbl-typology)."
    )

sentences.append(
    f"The distribution of {country}'s ecosystem types is shown in "
    "@fig-ecosystem-map, with each ecosystem in a distinct color."
)

print(" ".join(sentences))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-typology
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "List of the terrestrial ecosystems of {{< meta country-name >}} developed in this project."
# With a functional-group column, show the full GET typology (realm / biome /
# functional group / ecosystem). Otherwise show a plain ecosystem list.
if has_functional_group:
    # Drop the geometry column: its raw WKB/WKT is very long and adds nothing to
    # this list of ecosystem types (iucn_get_data also excludes it defensively).
    typology_df = ecosystems_df.drop(columns="geometry", errors="ignore")
    typology = iucn_get_data.Typology(
        language="spanish",
        ecosystems=typology_df,
        ecosystems_functional_group_column=functional_group_column,
        ecosystems_column=ecosystem_column,
        ecosystem_name_column=ecosystem_name_column,
    )
    display(typology)
else:
    # De-duplicate in case the code and name columns are the same (no separate
    # code column), so the same column is not selected twice.
    cols = list(dict.fromkeys(c for c in (ecosystem_column, ecosystem_name_column) if c))
    display(
        ecosystems_df[cols].drop_duplicates().reset_index(drop=True)
    )
```


```{python}
#| label: tbl-ecosystem-index
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Ecosystem index for {{< meta country-name >}}. Each index is the pixel value used for that ecosystem in the rasterized ecosystem map (COG)."
# The canonical index -> code -> name table, written by build_ecosystem_index.py.
# It is the legend for interpreting ecosystem COG pixel values.
import pandas as pd

index_csv = os.path.join('..', 'config', 'ecosystems', 'index.csv')
if os.path.exists(index_csv):
    display(pd.read_csv(index_csv))
else:
    print('(Ecosystem index has not been built yet — run `pixi run build-ecosystems`.)')
```


```{python}
#| label: display-ecosystem-map
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
# All-ecosystems distribution map. Reuses the same browser COG viewer as the
# per-ecosystem assessment pages (assets/cog-viewer.mjs, deck.gl COGLayer via
# esm.sh), but in categorical mode: each ecosystem index gets its own color.
# The coloring dimension is set by `map_color_by` in the country config
# (default: one color per ecosystem; `functional_group` groups ecosystems by
# their functional group). All grouping happens here in Python — the viewer
# module only maps pixel value -> color from the palette we pass it. Emitted as
# a Quarto figure Div so @fig-ecosystem-map resolves while the <script> runs.
import json
import colorsys

country = config['country_name']
raster_cfg = config.get('ecosystem_raster') or {}
cog_url = raster_cfg.get('cog_url')
color_by = config.get('map_color_by', 'ecosystem')

if not cog_url:
    print(
        "::: {.callout-note}\n"
        "The ecosystem map raster (COG) has not been built yet. Run "
        "`python scripts/rasterize_ecosystem_to_cog.py` to generate and "
        "publish it, then re-render.\n"
        ":::"
    )
else:
    # index -> code, in the same natural-sort order to_raster() used, so the
    # 1-based position equals the COG pixel value. Prefer the built index.csv;
    # fall back to unique_ecosystems() when it has not been generated yet.
    index_csv = os.path.join('..', 'config', 'ecosystems', 'index.csv')
    if os.path.exists(index_csv):
        idx_df = pd.read_csv(index_csv)
        index_to_code = {int(r['index']): str(r['code']) for _, r in idx_df.iterrows()}
    else:
        index_to_code = {i: str(c) for i, c in enumerate(eco.unique_ecosystems(), start=1)}

    # Choose the grouping dimension. functional_group needs a real FG column;
    # otherwise fall back to per-ecosystem coloring with a note.
    fallback_note = None
    code_to_group = None
    if color_by == 'functional_group':
        if has_functional_group:
            code_to_group = (
                ecosystems_df.drop_duplicates(ecosystem_column)
                .set_index(ecosystem_column)[functional_group_column]
                .astype(str)
                .to_dict()
            )
        else:
            fallback_note = (
                "`map_color_by: functional_group` was set, but no functional-group "
                "column is configured — coloring by ecosystem instead."
            )
            color_by = 'ecosystem'

    # Group key per ecosystem index (the ecosystem code itself, or its FG value).
    index_group = {}
    for i, code in index_to_code.items():
        index_group[i] = code_to_group.get(code, code) if code_to_group is not None else code

    # Distinct color per unique group (golden-ratio HSV -> RGB), assigned in the
    # first-appearance order of the natural-sorted indices for stability.
    GOLDEN, SAT, VAL, ALPHA = 0.6180339887498949, 0.65, 0.95, 200
    group_color = {}
    for i in sorted(index_group):
        g = index_group[i]
        if g not in group_color:
            k = len(group_color)
            r, gg, b = colorsys.hsv_to_rgb((k * GOLDEN) % 1.0, SAT, VAL)
            group_color[g] = [round(r * 255), round(gg * 255), round(b * 255), ALPHA]

    # palette indexed by pixel value; slot 0 and any gap stay transparent.
    max_index = max(index_group) if index_group else 0
    palette = [[0, 0, 0, 0] for _ in range(max_index + 1)]
    for i, g in index_group.items():
        palette[i] = group_color[g]

    if fallback_note:
        print("::: {.callout-note}\n" + fallback_note + "\n:::\n")

    module_src = "../assets/cog-viewer.mjs"  # overview page is one level deep
    viewer_opts = {"url": cog_url, "palette": palette, "height": 520}
    div_and_script = (
        '<div id="ecosystem-overview-cog-viewer" style="height:520px;width:100%;"></div>\n'
        '<script type="module">\n'
        f'import mount from "{module_src}";\n'
        f'mount(document.getElementById("ecosystem-overview-cog-viewer"), {json.dumps(viewer_opts)});\n'
        '</script>'
    )
    # Figure Div: last paragraph is the caption; @fig-ecosystem-map references it.
    print(
        "::: {#fig-ecosystem-map}\n"
        + div_and_script + "\n\n"
        + f"Distribution of the ecosystem types of {country}, each ecosystem "
        + "drawn in a distinct color, rendered client-side from the published "
        + "ecosystem COG.\n"
        + ":::"
    )

    # Legend: one row per color group, from the same group_color map so the
    # swatches match the map exactly. Scrollable so it stays compact for
    # countries with many ecosystems.
    names = eco.ecosystem_names() if ecosystem_name_column else {}
    if color_by == 'functional_group':
        seen = []
        for i in sorted(index_group):
            g = index_group[i]
            if g not in seen:
                seen.append(g)
        legend_rows = [(g, str(g)) for g in seen]
    else:
        legend_rows = []
        for i in sorted(index_to_code):
            code = index_to_code[i]
            label = code + (f" — {names[code]}" if code in names else "")
            legend_rows.append((code, label))

    swatches = "".join(
        '<div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;'
        'font:12px system-ui,sans-serif;">'
        '<span style="flex:0 0 auto;width:14px;height:14px;border:1px solid #999;'
        f'background:rgb({group_color[g][0]},{group_color[g][1]},{group_color[g][2]});">'
        '</span>'
        f'<span>{label}</span></div>'
        for g, label in legend_rows
    )
    print(
        '\n<div style="max-height:260px;overflow:auto;margin-top:8px;'
        'display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(220px,1fr));'
        f'gap:2px 12px;">{swatches}</div>'
    )
```


::: {.content-hidden unless-meta="show-instructions"}

:::: {.callout-important}
## How to customize the Ecosystem Overview page

- To modify the ecosystem overview text, edit the `content/2_ecosystem_overview.qmd` file.
- To remove the ecosystem overview section, edit the `_quarto.yml` file and remove the
  `- content/2_ecosystem_overview.qmd` line.

**To hide these instructions on all pages, set `show-instructions: false`
in the `_quarto.yml` file.**
::::
:::
